title: Adicea anomala
taxonomy: Adicea anomala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes over 13,000 species, ranging from trees and shrubs to herbs and climbers. Adicea anomala is native to Madagascar, an island country off the coast of East Africa. The plant is part of the Rubia genus, which is often referred to as the madder genus. Further classification includes the species being part of the tribe Rubieae, which consists of various species of flowering plants.
